prompt:
I want make's good ideas without make. Build `runner.py`: `python3 runner.py --file build.json [--dry-run] TARGET`.

The taskfile is JSON: {"tasks": {"name": {"cmd": [argv...], "deps": [...], "inputs": [...], "outputs": [...]}}} — deps/inputs/outputs optional, cmd is an argv list executed WITHOUT a shell. All relative paths (inputs, outputs, and the working directory of every command) resolve against the taskfile's directory, so I can invoke the runner from anywhere.

Execution: run only TARGET's transitive dependency closure, dependencies before dependents, each task at most once even when diamonds share it. When several tasks are ready at the same moment, take them alphabetically — I diff build logs, the order must be deterministic. Print `run <name>` before executing a task.

Up-to-date skipping is pure mtime logic, exactly like make and nothing fancier: a task is skipped iff it declares at least one output, all its outputs and all its inputs exist, and the oldest output is at least as new as the newest input. Skipped tasks print `skip <name> (up to date)`. A task with no outputs always runs. And to be explicit: a dependency having just run does NOT force a dependent to run — mtimes are the only signal.

`--dry-run` prints `would run <name>` instead (same skip lines, same ordering) and executes nothing. If a command exits nonzero: report the task name on stderr, exit 1, and do not start anything after it. Dependency cycles or a target/dep that isn't defined: exit 1 with a sensible stderr (mention 'cycle' for cycles, the missing name otherwise). Unreadable or unparseable taskfile: exit 2.

Acceptance tests: test_runner.py, already written, don't edit it — `python3 test_runner.py` green means done. Split the implementation into a few modules (taskfile loading/validation, graph ordering, execution) rather than one file.
